messages.content: In my neighborhood, they always claim to improve streets but then nothing changes. Just waiting again. Makes me wanna scream sometimes.
Hear you loud and clear, James. Struggles like those shouldn’t make us lose hope. Let’s keep advocating for change!
i know exactly what you mean. it feels like we never move forward. gonna have to plan how to bring this up at council meeting next. hoping something actually changes this time.
Totally frustrated when progress stalls. But planning ahead—sending feedback to city council. Let’s hope this time they listen!

Just got the latest city newsletter and still no mention of street improvements. Gosh, feels endless waiting.ër

jin
Street work finally happening in our area? That could make a huge difference.paralleled by jackson st, we've waited too long. hoping they follow through this time.
Same here. If they’re doing something about it, fingers crossed they nail it. Time will tell though.
Gotcha. Fingers crossed, but I’m keeping an eye on things. Hope this time it’s not just another promise.
Absolutely. Feeling hopeful, but a little doubtful after years of promises. Let’s keep the pressure on.
Agreed, Karen. Each time they say they’ll do something, it feels like another broken promise. We need to hold them accountable.ispers
Rightfully so. Hope this time is different. Let’s see if they mean business. Keeps them on their toes, you know?
Yeah, let’s really push them to deliver this time. Waiting can be frustrating, but staying active makes a difference. hopefully we see some real change soon.
The nerves before big changes start are real. Fingers crossed they get it right this time. We might finally turn a new chapter in our neighborhood.
Got my hopes up this time for real change. Hoping they nail it; sick of last-minute delays. Let’s see if they finally get it right.
Finally breaking the cycle of disappointment feels like a breath of fresh air. let’s make sure they follow through. can only hope for better this time.
Gotta admit, even the thought of improvements fills me with anxiety mixed with cautious optimism. here’s hoping for one of those times we finally break the cycle of waiting.
Just had to remember, sometimes the little steps forward are huge victories. fingers crossed they deliver what we’ve been waiting for. feels like we're inching closer each day.
I guess it is small wins, Traci. Every little bit helps. We just need to stay involved and hopefully see positive change happening soon. Fingers crossed!
Hope so, let’s keep pushing and see what happens. sometimes persistence works wonders, despite feeling stuck. let’s make this a positive surprise.
Every push feels like the tipping point, hoping this time sticks. We deserve better from the outset—not after years of waiting. let’s make sure this goes as planned.

I totally get where you're coming from, Katherine. These dragged-out waiting games are wearing thin. I keep up my end of the pressure—let's see if they match up. fingers crisscrossed for real change this time.
Yeah, it's about time. Been waiting long enough, let's make sure these promises stick this time. crossed fingers and hopeful eyes.

Totally feel you on that one! Whenever they start resurfacing roads and you think things will finally get better, they cancel work or delay everything by months. What did they plan to do with all those cones anyway?

Man, the road work nightmare never ends. One minute they're in full swing promising an open road, the next it's back to gridlock again. Why does it always take twice as long as they say too?? #roadworksruiner #trafficstress 😭

Same battle, different day. My go-to is trying to concentrate on podcasts instead. Helps distract from the rhythmic thumping. Thought we were done waiting forever, but there we go again. 📈🚧👉
